title: Releases now reject new files after 14 days - The Python Package Index Blog
excerpt: PyPI no longer allows publishing new files to releases older than 14 days.
content_html:
security

The Python Package Index (PyPI) now rejects new files being uploaded to releases that are older than 14 days. This restriction was put in place to prevent old and long-stable releases from being poisoned in case publishing tokens or workflows of PyPI projects were compromised. As far as we are aware this has not yet been abused, but there is no technical reason beyond that attackers weren't aware it was possible.

Users should not yet rely on this behavior as there are no defined semantics for "releases no longer accepting new files" or APIs available to confirm the state of the release. Instead, these semantics will be defined once "Upload 2.0 API" and "Staged Previews" have been standardized by PEP 694.

The discussion of this behavior began during PEP 740 (Digital Attestations) back in January 2024. The discussion was restarted in March 2026 after the popular packages LiteLLM and Telnyx were compromised. These packages were compromised due to a "mutable reference" in these projects' usage of the Trivy GitHub Action.

Originally the discussion stalled due to some projects depending on this behavior to add support for new Python versions to already-published releases. To quantify how disruptive this change would be to existing workflows, the PyPI database was queried for projects that have published new files to old releases (bucketed by number of days since the release). Later, specifically cp314 wheels were queried for the top 15,000 packages, revealing that only 56 projects of 15,000 had published a 3.14-compatible wheel more than 14 days after a release was available.

This topic was brought to the Packaging Summit at PyCon US 2026 by PyPI Safety & Security Engineer, Mike Fiedler. The rough consensus of the discussion was that the summit attendees thought it was "acceptable to require users to bump to the next version" to support new Python versions. With the data and consensus in hand, Seth moved forward with a patch to reject new files on old releases which was merged July 8th, 2026.

This change will protect Python users and reduce the amount of "cleanup" work associated with project compromises for PyPI admins. This restriction also means that compromises don't put releases into an indeterminate and confusing state of both "compromised" and "not compromised", where only a subset of files could be poisoned with malware. Stay tuned for the future where Upload 2.0 API provides semantics for releases that are "closed" instead of "open".

Seth Larson and Mike Fiedler's work at the Python Software Foundation is supported by Alpha-Omega.

sentences.zh: 掌握了数据和共识后，Seth 继续推出补丁以拒绝旧版本上的新文件，该补丁于 2026 年 7 月 8 日合并。

sentences.en: This change will protect Python users and reduce the amount of "cleanup" work associated with project compromises for PyPI admins.
sentences.zh: 此更改将保护 Python 用户并减少 PyPI 管理员与项目泄露相关的“清理”工作量。

sentences.en: This restriction also means that compromises don't put releases into an indeterminate and confusing state of both "compromised" and "not compromised", where only a subset of files could be poisoned with malware.
sentences.zh: 此限制还意味着妥协不会将版本置于“妥协”和“未妥协”的不确定和混乱状态，在这种状态下，只有一部分文件可能受到恶意软件的毒害。

sentences.en: Stay tuned for the future where Upload 2.0 API provides semantics for releases that are "closed" instead of "open".
sentences.zh: 请继续关注未来，Upload 2.0 API 将为“封闭”而不是“开放”的版本提供语义。

blocks.html_en: Seth Larson and Mike Fiedler's work at the Python Software Foundation is supported by Alpha-Omega.

sentences.en: Seth Larson and Mike Fiedler's work at the Python Software Foundation is supported by Alpha-Omega.
sentences.zh: Seth Larson 和 Mike Fiedler 在 Python 软件基金会的工作得到了 Alpha-Omega 的支持。

content_html_zh:
security

Python 包索引 (PyPI) 现在拒绝将新文件上传到早于 14 天的版本。 实施此限制是为了防止旧的和长期稳定的版本在发布令牌或 PyPI 项目的工作流程受到损害的情况下受到毒害。 据我们所知，这还没有被滥用，但除了攻击者不知道这是可能的之外，没有任何技术原因。

用户尚不应该依赖此行为，因为没有定义“版本不再接受新文件”的语义，也没有可用于确认版本状态的 API。 相反，一旦 PEP 694 标准化“上传 2.0 API”和“分阶段预览”，就会定义这些语义。

对这种行为的讨论始于 2024 年 1 月的 PEP 740（数字证明）。 在热门软件包 LiteLLM 和 Telnyx 遭到泄露后，讨论于 2026 年 3 月重新启动。 由于这些项目使用 Trivy GitHub Action 时的“可变引用”，这些包遭到了破坏。

最初，讨论因某些项目依赖此行为来向已发布的版本添加对新 Python 版本的支持而陷入停滞。 为了量化这一变化对现有工作流程的破坏程度，我们在 PyPI 数据库中查询了已将新文件发布到旧版本的项目（按发布后的天数存储）。 后来，专门查询了 cp314 轮子的前 15,000 个软件包，结果显示，在发布后 14 天以上，15,000 个项目中只有 56 个项目发布了兼容 3.14 的轮子。

该主题由 PyPI 安全工程师 Mike Fiedler 在 PyCon US 2026 包装峰会上提出。 讨论的粗略共识是，峰会与会者认为“要求用户升级到下一个版本”以支持新的 Python 版本是可以接受的。 掌握了数据和共识后，Seth 继续推出补丁以拒绝旧版本上的新文件，该补丁于 2026 年 7 月 8 日合并。

此更改将保护 Python 用户并减少 PyPI 管理员与项目泄露相关的“清理”工作量。 此限制还意味着妥协不会将版本置于“妥协”和“未妥协”的不确定和混乱状态，在这种状态下，只有一部分文件可能受到恶意软件的毒害。 请继续关注未来，Upload 2.0 API 将为“封闭”而不是“开放”的版本提供语义。

Seth Larson 和 Mike Fiedler 在 Python 软件基金会的工作得到了 Alpha-Omega 的支持。
